summary: Well-structured additive plan that extends the existing polyhedraEdges/wireframePolyhedra pipeline with three new generators. The PolyhedronEdgeData interface serves as a clean integration contract, meaning new generators slot in without modifying the rendering path. The plan correctly identifies that subdivision-level capping (not post-generation truncation) is the right budget strategy for geodesic spheres.
architectural_strengths: PolyhedronEdgeData interface is the single integration contract — all three new generators output the same shape, so the downstream pipeline (geometry creation, subdivision for arcs, shader material) is untouched
GenerationMode as a separate type from PolyhedronShape is the correct design — modes are orthogonal to shapes and should not pollute the shape selection logic
Cube added to PolyhedronShape union but excluded from SHAPES array prevents accidental selection while enabling internal use by dual generator
Edge budget is enforced at generation time via maxGeodesicLevel() rather than post-generation truncation — this avoids partial/broken geometry
Nested and dual generators have inherently modest edge counts (≤120), so budget enforcement is only truly needed for geodesic mode
Three new files are additive — no existing generator logic is restructured

architectural_risks.risk: selectGenerationMode uses a second seed-derived roll, adding a new PRNG consumption point that could theoretically shift downstream seed-derived values if the PRNG is shared state
architectural_risks.mitigation: Plan specifies independent seed derivation (seed + ':wireframe:mode:' + i), which isolates from existing selectShape rolls. Verify implementation uses separate PRNG instances or distinct seed strings.

architectural_risks.risk: Adding maxEdgesPerShape to QualityProfile widens the interface — all consumers of QualityProfile must handle the new field
architectural_risks.mitigation: Only wireframePolyhedra.ts consumes this field. The plan correctly scopes it as an optional config parameter in WireframePolyhedraConfig.

architectural_risks.risk: Geodesic level 4 produces 7680 edges — if maxEdgesPerShape is misconfigured or bypassed, this could cause frame drops
architectural_risks.mitigation: Plan caps high tier at 1920 edges (level 3). Level 4 is only reachable if budget is explicitly set above 7680. The maxGeodesicLevel helper enforces the cap deterministically.

interface_compatibility.PolyhedronEdgeData: All three generators output this interface — no changes needed to the rendering pipeline
interface_compatibility.WireframePolyhedraConfig: Extended with optional generationMode and maxEdgesPerShape — backward compatible
interface_compatibility.QualityProfile: Extended with maxEdgesPerShape — requires updating all three tier definitions (low/medium/high)
interface_compatibility.ShaderMaterial: Unchanged — generators only affect geometry, not shader uniforms or attributes
interface_compatibility.subdivideEdges: Works on any edge-pair array regardless of source generator — compatible

performance_assessment.geodesic: Level 1=120, Level 2=480, Level 3=1920 edges — well within GPU budget for wireframe rendering. Tier-gated subdivision prevents runaway complexity.
performance_assessment.nested: Max 4 layers × 30 edges (icosahedron) = 120 edges — negligible cost
performance_assessment.generation_time: All generators use THREE.js built-in geometry constructors + EdgesGeometry — fast, runs once at init, not per-frame
performance_assessment.memory: Geometry disposal pattern from existing generatePolyhedronEdges should be replicated in all new generators

test_coverage_assessment: Comprehensive. Plan tests edge counts against deterministic formulas, position finiteness, PolyhedronEdgeData format compliance, tier gating, and budget compliance. Existing 40-test wireframePolyhedra suite provides regression safety. The only gap is absence of visual/shader integration tests, but this matches the existing test strategy.

dependency_analysis.US-054_dependency: Plan builds on polyhedraEdges.ts and wireframePolyhedra.ts which were delivered in US-054. The existing interfaces are stable and well-tested.
dependency_analysis.downstream_impact: No other systems depend on the internal structure of polyhedraEdges output. constellationLines.ts references wireframe positions but only reads mesh.geometry.attributes.position — format is unchanged.

modification_scope.blast_radius: Contained to wireframe subsystem. No impact on other visual systems, audio pipeline, seed generation, or UI.

recommendations: Ensure all three new generators replicate the geometry disposal pattern (base.dispose(), edges.dispose()) from the existing generatePolyhedronEdges to prevent WebGL memory leaks
Verify that the nested solid rotation offsets use the same seed-derivation pattern as shape selection to maintain determinism
Consider adding a smoke test that instantiates all four generation modes and verifies no WebGL errors — this catches shader/attribute mismatches early
